exercises.name: Barbell Curl
exercises.description: A classic bicep exercise using a barbell to isolate the biceps muscle.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart
Grip the barbell with hands shoulder-width apart, palms facing forward
Keep elbows stationary at your sides
Curl the weight upward toward your chest, squeezing your biceps
Lower the weight with control back to the starting position
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions
tips: Keep your core tight and avoid swinging
Control the negative (lowering) portion of the movement
Don't use momentum to lift the weight

exercises.name: Dumbbell Curl
exercises.description: A free-weight bicep exercise using dumbbells for unilateral arm development.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, dumbbells at your sides
Keep your elbows stationary and tucked to your sides
Curl both dumbbells upward simultaneously, rotating palms as you lift
Squeeze at the top and lower with control
Repeat for desired repetitions
tips: Use controlled movements, avoid momentum
Maintain neutral wrists throughout
Can be performed seated for strict form

exercises.name: Triceps Dips
exercises.description: A bodyweight exercise using parallel bars or bench to isolate the triceps.
instructions: Grip the dip bars with hands shoulder-width apart
Lower yourself by bending your elbows to about 90 degrees
Keep your body upright and elbows close to your sides
Press back up to the starting position
Repeat for desired repetitions
tips: Lean slightly forward if using a bench
Keep elbows in to emphasize triceps over chest
Can use resistance bands for assistance if needed

exercises.name: Skull Crushers
exercises.description: An isolation exercise for the triceps performed lying on a bench.
instructions: Lie on a flat bench with barbell or dumbbells held above your chest
Lower the weight toward your forehead by bending at the elbows
Keep elbows stationary and pointed forward
Extend back to the starting position
Repeat for desired repetitions
tips: Use moderate weight to avoid injury
Keep elbows from flaring outward
Full range of motion is important

exercises.name: Push-ups
exercises.description: A fundamental bodyweight exercise that targets the chest, triceps, and shoulders.
instructions: Start in a plank position with hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart
Lower your body until your chest nearly touches the floor
Keep your elbows close to your body at roughly 45 degrees
Push through your hands to return to the starting position
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions
tips: Maintain a straight line from head to heels
Breathe in on the way down, exhale on the way up
If too difficult, start on your knees

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.description: A machine-based exercise for lower body strength development.
instructions: Sit in the leg press machine with your back and head resting against the pad
Place your feet on the platform about shoulder-width apart
Lower the platform by bending your knees to about 90 degrees
Push through your heels to extend your legs and return to the starting position
Avoid locking out your knees at the top
tips: Keep your back flat against the pad
Don't let your knees cave inward
Start with a moderate weight and focus on form

exercises.name: Deadlift
exercises.description: A complex compound movement that targets the posterior chain.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, toes under the barbell
Grip the bar just outside your legs with a shoulder-width grip
Keep your chest up and core tight as you initiate the lift
Drive through your heels while maintaining a straight back
Once the bar passes your knees, drive your hips forward
Lower the bar by hinging at the hips and bending your knees
tips: Keep the barbell close to your body throughout the movement
Engage your lats before lifting
This is an advanced lift - learn proper form before adding weight

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
exercises.description: A free-weight exercise for chest development with increased range of motion.
instructions: Lie on a flat bench with dumbbells held at chest level
Press the dumbbells upward and slightly inward until arms are nearly extended
Lower the dumbbells with control back to chest level
The dumbbells should travel in an arc, not straight up and down
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions
tips: Keep your feet flat on the floor for stability
Lower the dumbbells to where they're level with your chest
Maintain a slight bend in your elbows at the top

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown
exercises.description: A machine exercise that isolates and builds lat strength.
instructions: Sit at the lat pulldown machine with your thighs under the pads
Grip the bar slightly wider than shoulder-width
Pull the bar down toward your upper chest by driving your elbows down
Squeeze your lats at the bottom of the movement
Allow the bar to return to the starting position with control
tips: Lean back slightly at about 20 degrees
Avoid using too much momentum
Think about pulling your elbows down rather than pulling your hands

exercises.name: Seated Row
exercises.description: A machine exercise for back development and lat strengthening.
instructions: Sit with your chest against the pad and feet firmly on the floor
Grip the handles with a neutral grip
Pull the handles toward your torso by driving your elbows back
Squeeze your back muscles at the end of the movement
Allow the weight to return with control
tips: Keep your chest upright against the pad
Focus on pulling with your back, not your arms
Avoid rounding your shoulders forward

exercises.name: Bent Over Row
exercises.description: A compound movement that strengthens the entire back including upper back and lats.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart, barbell in front of you
Bend forward at the hips until your torso is nearly parallel to the ground
Grip the barbell slightly wider than shoulder-width
Pull the bar up to your lower chest/upper abdomen
Lower with control and repeat
tips: Keep your back straight throughout the movement
Let the weight pull your arms down between reps
Can be performed with dumbbells for each hand

exercises.name: Squats
exercises.description: A fundamental compound movement that works the entire lower body.
instructions: Stand with feet shoulder-width apart, barbell on your upper back
Keep your chest up and core engaged
Descend by bending at the hips and knees simultaneously
Lower until your thighs are parallel to the ground
Drive through your heels to return to the starting position
tips: Maintain neutral spine throughout the movement
Knees should track over your toes
Practice bodyweight squats before adding a barbell

exercises.name: Plank
exercises.description: An isometric core exercise that builds stability and endurance.
instructions: Start on your forearms and toes in a push-up position
Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels
Engage your core and avoid letting your hips sag
Hold this position for the desired amount of time
Maintain steady breathing throughout
tips: Start with 20-30 seconds and gradually increase duration
Focus on form over time
Your shoulders should be directly over your elbows

exercises.name: Crunches
exercises.description: A core isolation exercise that targets the upper abdominal muscles.
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor
Place your hands behind your head or across your chest
Curl your shoulders upward by contracting your abs
Lower back down with control
Repeat for desired repetitions
tips: Avoid pulling on your head - let your abs do the work
Move through a partial range of motion
Keep your feet planted on the floor

exercises.name: Leg Raises
exercises.description: A lower abdominal exercise that targets the lower portion of the rectus abdominis.
instructions: Lie on your back with legs straight and arms at your sides
Keep your lower back pressed to the floor
Raise both legs up to about 90 degrees
Lower your legs back down without letting them touch the floor
Repeat for desired repetitions
tips: Keep your lower back pressed to prevent injury
Move slowly and under control
Can bend knees for easier variation

exercises.name: Russian Twists
exercises.description: A rotational core exercise that emphasizes the oblique muscles.
instructions: Sit on the floor with knees bent and feet slightly lifted
Hold a weight to your chest and lean back slightly
Rotate your torso to one side, bringing the weight toward the floor
Return to center and repeat on the other side
Alternate sides for desired repetitions
tips: Keep your chest up and core engaged
Move in a controlled manner
Increase range of motion gradually

exercises.name: Side Plank
exercises.description: A lateral core exercise that emphasizes the oblique muscles and side stabilizers.
instructions: Lie on your side with your elbow directly under your shoulder
Stack your feet or stagger them for easier variation
Raise your hips until your body forms a straight line
Hold this position for the desired amount of time
Repeat on the other side
tips: Keep your body in a straight line
Don't let your hips sag or pike upward
Start with shorter durations and increase gradually

exercises.name: Hip Thrust
exercises.description: A powerful glute isolation exercise that effectively targets the gluteus maximus.
instructions: Sit with your upper back against a bench or elevated surface
Place a weight across your hips or hold it at your chest
Place your feet flat on the ground hip-width apart
Drive through your heels and extend your hips upward
Squeeze your glutes at the top and lower back down
tips: Get a full range of motion from bottom to top
Squeeze your glutes hard at the peak
Can start with bodyweight before adding resistance

exercises.name: Glute Bridges
exercises.description: A foundational glute exercise that targets the gluteus maximus and posterior chain.
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat on the floor
Place your feet directly under your knees
Drive through your heels and lift your hips toward the ceiling
Squeeze your glutes at the top of the movement
Lower back down and repeat
tips: Keep your shoulders and head on the ground
Drive through your heels, not your toes
Maintain a neutral spine throughout

exercises.name: Clamshells
exercises.description: An isolation exercise for the gluteus medius and hip abductors.
instructions: Lie on your side with knees bent at about 90 degrees
Keep your feet together and hips stacked
Lift your top knee upward while keeping feet together
Lower back down and repeat
tips: Don't let your hips roll backward
Move slowly and control the movement
Can add a resistance band around the knees

exercises.name: Back Extension
exercises.description: A lower back isolation exercise using a machine for safe spinal extension.
instructions: Sit in the back extension machine with your upper back against the pad
Secure the footplate with your legs
Extend your back by pushing against the resistance
Return to the starting position with control
Repeat for desired repetitions
tips: Keep your core tight throughout
Move through a full range of motion
Don't hyperextend your lower back

exercises.name: Good Mornings
exercises.description: A compound movement that strengthens the lower back and posterior chain.
instructions: Stand with a barbell across your shoulders like you're doing a squat
Keep your legs mostly straight with a slight knee bend
Hinge at the hips and lower your torso toward the ground
Keep your back straight and return to upright
Repeat for desired repetitions
tips: Start with very light weight to learn the movement
Keep your back straight - don't round
Limited range of motion is fine for safety

exercises.name: Hip Flexor Raises
exercises.description: An exercise that targets the hip flexor muscles including the iliopsoas and sartorius.
instructions: Stand upright with your feet hip-width apart
Keep your core engaged and back straight
Lift one knee up toward your chest as high as comfortable
Lower back down and repeat on the other leg
Alternate sides for desired repetitions
tips: Move in a controlled manner
Keep your core engaged throughout
Can hold onto a support for balance if needed

exercises.name: Wrist Curls
exercises.description: An isolation exercise for the forearm muscles, focusing on wrist flexion.
instructions: Sit on a bench with your forearms resting on your thighs
Hold a dumbbell in each hand with palms facing upward
Lower the weight by curling your wrists downward
Raise back up by curling your wrists upward
Repeat for desired repetitions
tips: Use light weight and focus on the forearms
Full range of motion is important
Can also be performed with palms facing down for reverse curls

exercises.name: Farmer's Carry
exercises.description: A compound exercise that builds grip strength and forearm endurance by walking with heavy weights.
instructions: Stand with a heavy dumbbell or kettlebell in each hand
Keep your shoulders packed and core engaged
Walk forward for a set distance or time
Lower the weights and rest
Repeat for desired sets
tips: Maintain an upright posture
Start with moderate weight to focus on form
Can increase distance or weight gradually

exercises.name: Dumbbell Shoulder Press
exercises.description: An overhead pressing movement that builds shoulder strength.
instructions: Stand or sit with dumbbells held at shoulder height, palms facing forward
Press the dumbbells overhead until your arms are nearly fully extended
Lower the dumbbells back to shoulder height with control
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions
tips: Engage your core to protect your lower back
Don't lock out your elbows completely at the top
Use a stable stance if standing

exercises.name: Calf Raises
exercises.description: An exercise that isolates the calf muscles for lower leg strength.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart
Rise up on your toes by contracting your calf muscles
Hold at the top for a brief moment and squeeze your calves
Lower your heels back to the ground
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions
tips: Move slowly and controlled
For added difficulty, hold dumbbells
You should feel a strong stretch at the bottom position

exercises.name: Hip Flexor Stretch
exercises.description: A stretching exercise that targets the hip flexor muscles.
instructions: Stand in a lunge position with one leg forward, knee bent
Keep the back leg straight and push the hips forward
Feel the stretch in the front hip of the back leg
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds
Repeat on the other side
tips: Keep your back leg straight for a deeper stretch
Lean forward slightly to intensify the stretch
Breathe deeply and avoid bouncing

exercises.name: Hamstring Stretch
exercises.description: A stretching exercise that targets the hamstring muscles.
instructions: Sit with one leg extended and the other bent
Reach toward the toes of the extended leg
Keep your back straight and lean from the hips
Hold the stretch for 20-30 seconds
Repeat on the other side
tips: Keep your knee slightly bent if you're tight
Avoid rounding your back
Feel the stretch down the back of your thigh

exercises.name: Quad Stretch
exercises.description: A stretching exercise that targets the quadriceps muscles.
instructions: Stand on one leg and pull the other foot toward your glutes
Keep your knees close together
Hold for 20-30 seconds
Repeat on the other side
tips: Keep your knees aligned for proper form
Hold onto something for balance if needed
Don't pull your foot to the side

exercises.name: Calf Stretch
exercises.description: A stretching exercise that targets the calf muscles.
instructions: Step forward with one leg and place your hands on your thigh
Keep your back leg straight with heel on the ground
Lean forward to stretch the calf
Hold for 20-30 seconds
Repeat on the other side
tips: Keep your back heel firmly on the ground
Lean forward to increase the stretch
You should feel the stretch down the back of your lower leg

exercises.name: Chest Stretch
exercises.description: A stretching exercise that targets the chest muscles.
instructions: Stand in a doorway with one arm raised to 90 degrees
Place your forearm on the doorframe
Step forward to stretch your chest
Hold for 20-30 seconds
Repeat on the other side
tips: Keep your shoulders relaxed
Don't force the stretch
Feel the stretch across your chest and shoulder

exercises.name: Shoulder Cross-Body Stretch
exercises.description: A stretching exercise that targets the shoulder muscles.
instructions: Stand with good posture
Pull one arm across your body using your other arm
Pull at the elbow to stretch the shoulder
Hold for 20-30 seconds
Repeat on the other side
tips: Keep your shoulders relaxed
Pull at the elbow, not the wrist
Feel the stretch in the back of your shoulder

exercises.name: Triceps Overhead Stretch
exercises.description: A stretching exercise that targets the triceps muscles.
instructions: Stand with good posture
Raise one arm overhead and bend at the elbow
Use your other hand to gently pull the elbow back
Hold for 20-30 seconds
Repeat on the other side
tips: Keep your shoulders relaxed
Avoid pulling too hard
Feel the stretch down the back of your arm

exercises.name: Lower Back Stretch
exercises.description: A stretching exercise that targets the lower back muscles.
instructions: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat
Pull one knee toward your chest
Hold for 20-30 seconds
Repeat on the other side
tips: Don't force the stretch
Keep your head and shoulders relaxed
Feel the stretch in your lower back

exercises.name: Lat Stretch
exercises.description: A stretching exercise that targets the latissimus dorsi muscles.
instructions: Stand with feet hip-width apart
Reach one arm overhead and to the side
Lean your torso toward the raised arm
Hold for 20-30 seconds
Repeat on the other side
tips: Keep both feet on the ground
Feel the stretch down the side of your body
Avoid leaning backward or forward

exercises.name: Glute Stretch
exercises.description: A stretching exercise that targets the glute muscles.
instructions: Lie on your back with both knees bent and feet flat
Place one ankle on the opposite knee, forming a figure-4
Pull the bottom leg toward your chest
Hold for 20-30 seconds
Repeat on the other side
tips: Feel the stretch in your glute area
Keep your head and shoulders relaxed
Don't force the stretch
